section: 6.3.1 Observe and identify appearance of materials
learningObjective: Distinguish between genuine metals and non-metals that only appear metallic due to coatings (paint, lacquer, foil, wax, polish). Understand the scratch-test rule: only a metal is metal all the way through.
coatingRule: A coating (paint, lacquer, foil, wax) can make any material look lustrous. The scratch test reveals the truth — only a metal is metal all the way through.

items.name: Brass Utensil
items.description: A shiny brass bowl used in the kitchen — it clangs when tapped.
items.correctReason: ✅ Brass IS a metal (an alloy of copper + zinc). Its shine is natural lustre, not a coating.
items.wrongHint: 💡 Brass is a real metal — an alloy of copper and zinc. The shine is genuine lustre!

items.name: Silver-Painted Plastic Bangle
items.description: Looks exactly like a silver bangle — shiny, smooth, cool to touch.
items.correctReason: ✅ NOT a metal! Scratch the surface and you find plastic underneath. The silver look is just paint.
items.wrongHint: 💡 Looks metallic, but 'all that glitters is not gold'! Scratch it — you'll find plastic under the silver paint.

items.name: Chocolate Foil Wrapper
items.description: The shiny wrapper from a chocolate bar — thin, crinkly, reflective.
items.correctReason: ✅ NOT a metal! The outer shiny layer is metallic foil, but scratch it and you reach paper or plastic underneath.
items.wrongHint: 💡 The top layer looks metallic, but most chocolate wrappers are paper or plastic with a thin foil coating — not a solid metal!

items.name: Copper Wire
items.description: A reddish-brown wire found inside electrical cables — flexible and shiny when cut.
items.correctReason: ✅ Copper IS a metal — it conducts electricity because of its metallic properties. Real lustre on freshly cut surface.
items.wrongHint: 💡 Copper is a genuine metal! Its reddish lustre and excellent electrical conduction are classic metal properties.

items.name: Polished Plastic Tumbler
items.description: A very shiny tumbler — looks almost metallic, feels lighter than it looks.
items.correctReason: ✅ NOT a metal! Polishing gives plastic a shiny appearance, but scratch the surface — it's plastic all the way through.
items.wrongHint: 💡 Polishing makes plastic look metallic! But coating does not change what's inside. Scratch test → plastic.

items.name: Steel Key
items.description: A house key — heavy, hard, makes a ringing sound when dropped.
items.correctReason: ✅ Steel IS a metal (iron + carbon alloy). Sonorous, hard, and lustrous — classic metal properties.
items.wrongHint: 💡 A steel key is a real metal! Its weight, hardness, and ringing sound are all metal properties.

items.name: Gold-Coloured Paper Crown
items.description: A shiny gold party crown — it sparkles just like real gold jewellery.
items.correctReason: ✅ NOT a metal! It's paper coated with gold-coloured foil or paint. Fold it — paper creases, metal bends.
items.wrongHint: 💡 Gold colour ≠ gold metal! This is paper with a gold coating. Fold it firmly — paper folds, true metal would deform differently.

items.name: Lacquered Wooden Box
items.description: A beautifully finished wooden box — its lacquered surface shines like polished metal.
items.correctReason: ✅ NOT a metal! Lacquer (varnish coating) creates the shine. The core is wood — scratch it and you see the grain.
items.wrongHint: 💡 Lacquer is a shiny coating applied to wood. It looks lustrous, but the material underneath is wood — not metal!

desktop.layout: centred card, side-by-side buttons, full animations

quizStructure.interaction: Click Metal or Not Metal button on each screen
quizStructure.wrongBehavior: shake animation + amber hint banner + Try Again button (question stays)
quizStructure.correctBehavior: green lock + correct reason banner + Next Item / See Results button
quizStructure.progressTracking: 8-dot progress bar in header (amber=current, green=correct, red=wrong)
quizStructure.summary: Trophy, stats grid (correct/wrong/time), per-item review list, Coating Rule callout, Try Again

studentInstructions: Decide if each item is metal or not. A coating can trick you — read the reason after each answer.
errorAlertKey: Age alone does not decide shine; tarnish vs freshly cut. Also: coatings (paint, lacquer, foil, wax, polish) can make ANY material look metallic — scratch the surface to find the truth.
